regimeSub: CPI release today pivotal as Warsh Fed leans hawkish amid 57K NFP miss and renewed Middle East fighting.
themes: June CPI (today) expected to confirm sticky 4.2% YoY → markets pricing hawkish Warsh Fed hold, July FOMC minutes showed split on hikes, UST 10Y at 4.56% reflecting rate uncertainty

BOK minutes signal Thursday rate hike to 2.75% as new governor adopts hawkish posture → USDKRW eased to 1493 from 1505 but won remains under pressure, KOSPI -3.5% from June highs as SK Hynix whipsaws 13% ADR vs -15% local
Trump Iran ceasefire collapse July 9 + Spain trade cutoff → OPEC+ upped August quotas but Middle East fighting resumes, Brent spikes to $84 from $79, geopolitical risk premium rebuilding
June NFP 57K miss + Q1 GDP 2.1% soft + retail sales resilient → classic stagflation setup where labor cools but inflation persists, VIX subdued at 17.16 suggests complacency ahead of CPI print

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10Y nominal 4.56% less 4.2% CPI = 36bp real; compressed but positive as Warsh maintains hawkish bias

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 35bp steepening (2Y 4.21%, 10Y 4.56%) reflects term premium rebuild on fiscal/inflation concerns

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads contained despite stagflation setup; equity vol muted, credit complacent pre-CPI

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.16 low given CPI catalyst today + geopolitical flare; vol sellers overconfident ahead of data

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $84.18 up from $79 post-ceasefire collapse; OPEC+ quota hike offset by renewed ME fighting

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4016 near highs despite June selloff; inflation hedge bid returns as real rates compress

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: USDKRW 1493 easing from 1505 as BOK hike Thursday priced; still elevated on Fed-BOK divergence

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SK Hynix ADR +13% vs Korea -15% dislocation; TSMC earnings Thursday key for AI capex narrative

headline: Markets brace for June CPI print as Warsh Fed holds hawkish amid 57K payrolls miss, Trump's Iran ceasefire collapse, and BOK poised for Thursday hike to 2.75%.
